visual_features.overall_style: Conservative corporate layout with a minimalist, flat visual style. Strong brand presence through a solid blue top and bottom band, neutral content area, and restrained use of color. Very clean, text‑driven presentation with no decorative imagery in the viewed state.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is established through strong horizontal bands (blue header and footer), centered page title, and typographic contrast. The logo in the top-left and the primary navigation in the header form the first focal area. The next focal point is the centered page title 'AUTHOR ARCHIVES: ADMIN' (all caps, slightly larger size). Below that, the 'Nothing Found' heading draws attention as the main content heading, followed by the explanatory paragraph and the large full-width search input. The right sidebar is visually secondary, separated by a vertical divider and smaller headings. Footer text is lowest in hierarchy with light color and small size.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Generous vertical whitespace between header, page title, content, sidebar elements, and footer. Main content block and sidebar are given significant breathing room with ample padding inside the white content area and large top and bottom margins before the blue footer begins. Horizontal spacing between main content and sidebar is clear via a gap and subtle vertical divider.
unique_elements: Full-width solid blue header and footer bands framing a white central content canvas
Logo with integrated arrow motif using blue and red, reinforcing directional/progress symbolism
Centered page title in all caps placed between header and content, giving a clear contextual bar
Right sidebar separated from main content with a vertical line divider for clear column distinction

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit 12-column grid with a centered fixed-width content container. Main content likely spans 8–9 columns with a 3–4 column right sidebar.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Classic corporate layout with full-width header and footer, a centered content container, and a right sidebar. The page employs a two-column content area within a single main content band.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top solid header with logo and main navigation, followed by a single-line contextual page title centered on white background. Underneath is one main content section split into a wider left column and a narrower right sidebar. The left column contains heading, explanatory text, and a large search field. The right column contains a smaller search box and a 'Recent Comments' block. A large uniform footer spans the full width at the bottom.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Logo left-aligned, navigation right of logo in a horizontal row; language switch right-aligned in the header. Page title is center-aligned. Inside the content area, text in columns is left-aligned. Vertical alignment is built around a clear central axis for the main content container. Elements align to a consistent left margin inside each column.
responsive_hints: Full-width header and footer suggest they can adapt fluidly to any viewport width.
Centered content container hints at a max-width (likely 1080–1200px) with auto side margins, which would shrink gracefully on smaller screens.
Two-column content with sidebar would likely stack into a single column on smaller viewports, with the main content appearing above the sidebar.
Navigation as a single horizontal row indicates that a mobile breakpoint would probably convert this into a stacked or hamburger menu.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow: full-width blue header with logo and horizontal navigation, followed by a white area containing a centered page title. Beneath the title, the main content area is a two-column layout: the left column holds the 'Nothing Found' heading, descriptive paragraph, and a full-width search input; the right column holds a shorter search input and a 'Recent Comments' text block below it. The entire content area is then followed by a large full-width blue footer containing a single centered line of copyright text.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Header and footer bands occupy roughly ~15–20% of the vertical viewport combined, with the central white content taking the remaining ~80%. Within the content band, the left main content column visually occupies about 65–70% of the width while the right sidebar uses about 30–35%. Vertical spacing between title and main content is generous (approx. 40–60px). Inputs and headings have moderate spacing (approx. 16–24px) between them. The footer has large vertical padding, creating a solid block of brand color at the bottom.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Spacious layout with moderate content density. Text blocks are concise and surrounded by large white areas, and each functional element (search input, headings) has generous padding and margins. The sidebar is also very light in content with only a few elements, contributing to an overall low-density, clean, and uncluttered impression.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Strong vertical flow: the eye starts at the logo in the top-left then moves right across the navigation (a mild Z-pattern across the header). From there, the centered page title pulls the eye downward to the middle of the screen. The user’s gaze continues down to the 'Nothing Found' heading and explanatory text, then to the wide search input as the primary action element. The vertical divider nudges secondary attention to the right sidebar search box and 'Recent Comments'. Finally, the gaze drops to the footer, which visually closes the page with a solid blue band.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Buttons are implied through the search input’s submission area: a rectangular shape with sharp 0–2px corners, sharing the same height as the input field. Flat design with no gradient, no pronounced shadow, and a 1px light grey border. Primary CTAs would likely use the brand blue (#3654A4) fill with white uppercase text, while secondary actions may be outlined or plain text links. Padding horizontally is moderate (approx. 12–16px) with line-height aligned to input height (~36–40px).
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Implemented as <button> or <input type="submit"> directly adjacent to the input inside a form, often wrapped in a <form> with flex or inline-block display. For other CTAs, likely <a> elements styled as buttons for navigation or contextual actions.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit cards are visible, but content blocks behave like cardless sections: white background, 1px light grey borders or dividers between functional areas (e.g., sidebar sections, vertical divider). If product cards exist, they would likely be simple rectangles on white with subtle 1px borders and possibly a very soft box-shadow (e.g., rgba(0,0,0,0.05)) with 2–4px corner radius and internal padding of 16–24px.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected structure: a <div class="card"> wrapper containing an optional image/top element, a heading (<h3> or <h4>), short descriptive text, and possibly action links or buttons. Layout likely driven by flexbox or CSS grid in a multi-column card grid within the main content area.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Form elements are simple flat rectangles with sharp corners (0–2px radius). Inputs have a white fill, thin 1px light grey (#DDDDDD) border, and grey placeholder text. Focus state likely darkens the border (to a darker grey or blue) without heavy glow. Labels are not visible above the search fields; instead, placeholder text acts as the label, generating a clean but minimalistic form. Overall form density is low, with generous vertical spacing between fields (approx. 16–24px). Validation feedback is not shown but would likely use colored borders (red for error, green for success) and small inline messages underneath.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Forms use stacked, full-width inputs within a <form> element. For more complex forms, expected structure is label elements above or immediately before inputs in markup, even if placeholders visually serve as labels. Grouping may be done with wrapping <div class="form-group"> elements for each label-input pair, with optional helper or error text elements below.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary navigation is a horizontal menu on a solid blue background. Menu items are uppercase, white text with ample horizontal spacing (approx. 24–32px between items). Items are separated visually by whitespace and possibly thin vertical separators in text form. Dropdown indicators (for 'INTRODUCTION' and 'PRODUCTS') use small downward chevrons or triangles. Active state likely uses slightly bolder text, a different text color (e.g., light blue or white with underline), or a bottom border line. Hover states likely include color change (e.g., text to a lighter blue/white or background to a darker blue) while keeping the flat, no-shadow appearance.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Standard HTML unordered list <ul> with <li> items each containing <a> links. Dropdowns for multi-level navigation likely use nested <ul> within <li> elements. The language switch ('VI | EN') behaves like simple text links styled in the same bar, possibly aligned with float or flexbox to the far right.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Sidebar 'Recent Comments' list is very low-density: heading in uppercase bold, followed by a thin horizontal rule and large whitespace; list items (when present) would likely be single-line text links with minimal decoration. No visible bullets, relying instead on spacing and typography. Separator style is subtle: light grey rule lines or simple vertical spacing between items. No thumbnails are visible; it is a text-only list pattern. Hierarchy is conveyed via bold headings and regular-weight item text.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Unordered list (

    / elements. For more complex product lists, expected pattern would be a flexbox or grid-based 'media object' with optional thumbnail on the left and text on the right, or grid cards structured as repeated

heading.visual_style: Bold, clean sans-serif with relatively neutral proportions, good legibility, and slight squareness in letterforms, used in uppercase for navigation and section titles.

body.visual_style: Simple humanist or neo-grotesque sans-serif, optimized for readability at smaller sizes with moderate x-height and open counters.

spacing_system.component_padding: Typical components (inputs, card-like sections, navigation items) appear to use 8–16px inner padding on smaller elements (inputs, buttons) and 16–24px for larger blocks (sidebar sections, main content blocks).
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (header to title, title to content, content to footer) is around 32–48px, creating distinct vertical rhythm and clear separation.

interaction_hints: Navigation items likely change text color and/or background shade on hover, with a possible underline or bottom border for active state.
Search inputs probably show a slightly darker border and perhaps subtle inner shadow or focus ring on focus, adhering to accessible focus indication.
Dropdown menus for 'INTRODUCTION' and 'PRODUCTS' may use simple fade or slide-down animations, appearing directly beneath their parent menu items.
Links in content and sidebar likely change to the brand blue or underline on hover, with smooth color transitions (150–250ms) for a refined feel.
Buttons (e.g., search submit) may use a slight background darkening on hover and a subtle inset or darker outline on active/pressed state.
